text: The Crystal Spear is a Spear , a melee armament in Elden Ring .

title: Crystal Spear
text: Spear fashioned from pure crystal ; a deed impossible for a human . Enwreathed with powerful magic , its attack scales with intelligence . The inscrutable Crystalians have but one clear purpose; to safeguard their crystals unto the end. One theory posits that they yearn for the return of their creator who will carve for them new brethren.

title: Crystal Spear
text: Location : Sellia Hideaway , Caelid Found inside a chest behind an illusory wall in the large crystal-pierced room just before the tunnel leading to the boss room.

title: Crystal Spear
text: Crystal Knife Crystal Staff Crystal Sword Rotten Crystal Spear

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Death Ritual Spear — Type: Spear; Attack type: Pierce; Skill: Spearcall Ritual; FP cost: 20; Weight: 6.5; Sell price: 500; Tradeable: Yes

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Death Ritual Spear attribute requirements and scaling — Requires: Strength 14 (10 two-handed), Dexterity 20, Intelligence 18; Scaling: Strength E, Dexterity D, Intelligence D

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: The Death Ritual Spear is a Spear , a melee armament in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Effects 3 Skill 4 Acquisition 5 Upgrading 6 Notes 7 Trivia 8 See Also

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Ritual spear used by priests of old who were permitted to come among the Deathbirds . The priests became guardians of the birds through the rite of Death , which also serves as an oath sworn to their distant resurrection . Unique Skill : Spearcall Ritual Thrust the spear high, bearing prayers into the sky to summon a downpour of spectral spears.

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Weapon damage scales mostly with Dexterity while the skill scales with Intelligence . This weapon can't be buffed.

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Unique Skill: Spearcall Ritual This weapon can't be infused with Ashes of War .

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Location : Mountaintops of the Giants The Death Ritual Spear is dropped by the Death Rite Bird on the Mountaintops of the Giants , located on the cliffs just southwest of Castle Sol . It appears even during the day. The nearby Tibia Mariner drops Helphen's Steeple .

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: The Death Ritual Spear is strengthened to +10 with Somber Smithing Stones .

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: The Death Ritual Spear is a bladed spear with eight branches. It is a larger version of the Blue-Feathered and Red-Feathered Branchswords , also used by Servants of Death . Has the same horisontal slash heavy attack as the Partisan and the Spiked Spear . Be wary of the fact that the skill's damage comes from the explosions on the ground, not the fall of the spears. This means they will miss certain floating and jumping foes, such as the Dragonlord , or foes where the lock-on point isn't over a groundbound hitbox, such as phase 2 of Fire Giant .

title: Death Ritual Spear
text: Reference to the Seven-Branched Sword dating to the Yamato period . Patch 1.04 decreased the recovery time after using the skill. Patch 1.07 increased the damage of the skill and decreased the amount of time the damage is active. Patch 1.08 decreased poise damage of attacks in PvP. Patch 1.09 fixed an issue where certain attacks had the wrong Physical scaling, increased the speed of guard counters, and increased how quickly the spears appear.

title: Death Ritual Spear

title: Broadsword
text: Broadsword attribute requirements and scaling — Requires: Strength 10 (8 two-handed), Dexterity 10; Scaling: Strength D, Dexterity E

title: Broadsword
text: Broadsword is a straight sword found in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 See Also

title: Broadsword
text: A straight sword with a wide blade, suited to slashing attacks . Horizontal swing boast wide attack range, making it easier to connect with foes.

title: Broadsword
text: Starting equipment for the Confessor class. Nomadic Merchant southeast of Coastal Cave , beneath the collapsed structure.

title: Broadsword
text: The default skill is useful for breaking the guard of blocking enemies with the light attack version or for breaking their Stance with the heavy attack version. Always has the highest Attack Power (AP) of all regular Straight Swords, with the exception of the Quality Affinity, primarily due to its high base damage being able to compensate for its somewhat mediocre scaling. Since it's acquirable extremely early, as soon as the tutorial is completed, this weapon is a useful tool for potentially the entire game. Surprisingly, compared to its predecessors from previous game, this weapon isn't short but merely of average length. Leans towards Strength for Cold and Blood/Poison affinities. Despite having the highest damage and scaling on its Heavy affinity it actually gives slightly weaker Strength -scaling for "bullet"-type (i.e.

title: Broadsword
text: doesn't scale directly with the used weapon's damage) Skills with a Heavy, Quality, or Fire affinity, giving them usually about 2% less damage than they otherwise would have. This includes, as an example, the skills Ground Slam , Storm Blade , and Flame of the Redmanes (these skills however perform as normal on any affinity besides Heavy). Has a unique heavy attack and rolling attack, shared with the Cane Sword , replacing the usual thrusts with a broad, sweeping strike and an upward cut specifically. This is mostly a disadvantage. While the unique heavy attack is mostly good, it lacks the Pierce damage of the usual Straight Sword heavy attack and thus can't counter-hit attacking foes, while the unique rolling attack is very lacking in reach.

title: Broadsword
text: Upgraded with regular Smithing Stones . Can have its Affinity and Skill changed. All non-physical Affinities lower all types of guarded damage negation by 5%, except for the associated element where the damage negation increases by 25% and another element also increases by 15%. Magic negation receives the secondary 15% negation bonus on Sacred and Cold affinities, Fire negation gets the increase on Lightning affinity, Lightning negation gets the increase on Fire and Flame Art affinities, and Holy negation gets the increase on Magic affinity. The upper number in the Atk column denotes Physical base damage while the lower, colored number in the Atk column denotes elemental base damage: red for Fire damage, yellow for Lightning damage, orange for Holy damage, and cyan for Magic damage. See the Weapon Scaling page for what the scaling values mean.

title: Cane Sword
text: Cane Sword is a straight sword found in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 See Also

title: Cane Sword
text: Beloved sword of an aged knight whose last quest was long behind him, repurposed into a walking stick. The tip is rather worn. Though bent of back and without the brawn required even to raise the sword aloft, still he wished to meet his end with weapon in hand.

title: Cane Sword
text: Found next to the stairs just south of the West Capital Rampart Site of Grace in Leyndell, Royal Capital and Leyndell, Ashen Capital .

title: Cane Sword
text: The default skill is useful for breaking the guard of blocking enemies with the light attack version or for breaking their Stance with the heavy attack version. The weakest of all Straight Swords in the game, and of below average length on top of that. Slightly stronger on Dexterity -leaning affinities such as Keen. Despite this its Keen affinity actually gives it slightly weaker Dexterity-scaling for "bullet"-type (i.e. doesn't scale directly with the used weapon's damage) Skills with a Keen, Quality, Lightning, or Cold affinity, giving them usually about 2% less damage than they otherwise would have. This includes, as an example, the skills Beast's Roar , Storm Blade , Thunderbolt , and Hoarfrost Stomp (these skills however perform as normal on any affinity besides Keen).

title: Cane Sword
text: Has the same unique sweeping heavy attack and rolling attack as the Broadsword , and as such lacks attacks that deal Pierce damage when used on its own, only having the dual-wield backstep attack for that. The rolling attack does come out slightly quicker than the regular version.

title: Carian Knight's Sword

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: Carian Knight's Sword attribute requirements and scaling — Requires: Strength 10 (8 two-handed), Dexterity 10, Intelligence 18; Scaling: Strength D, Dexterity D, Intelligence D

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: Carian Knight's Sword is a straight sword found in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 See also

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: Straight sword embedded with a blue glintstone . Weapon of knights sworn to Carian royalty . These knights' sword could serve as catalysts , letting them wield sorcerous battle skills . Despite numbering fewer than twenty, this power made them a match for even the champions of gold in battle.

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: Held by a Carriage located in the northwest region of Liurnia of the Lakes , between the Northern Liurnia Lake Shore and The Four Belfries Sites of Grace .

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: The locked, innate skill, Carian Grandeur , while not unique and reliant on its weaker Intelligence -scaling, is still very strong if it can hit. Best used as a replacement for a Critical Attack or while lying in ambush since it charges somewhat slowly. Gains the most Attack Power (AP) from a hybrid Strength /Intelligence build (616 AP from having 45 Strength, 10 Dexterity , and 45 Intelligence), a bit less from a pure Strength build (609 AP from 80 Strength, 10 Dexterity, and 18 Intelligence), and far less from a pure Intelligence build (576 AP from 10 Strength, 10 Dexterity, and 80 Intelligence). Note that since the skill's damage is entirely reliant on the character's Intelligence and the weapon's Intelligence-scaling, the skill will be weaker on the pure Strength build compared to the other two builds.

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: Since the Carian Knight's Sword innate Intelligence-scaling is fairly poor, strengthening the skill and the sword's Attack Power together is somewhat difficult, with only the ease of upgrading weapons with Somber Smithing Stones over those needing Smithing Stones keeping it in front of regular Magic affinity weapons with this skill equipped. Has a unique blocking heavy attack, shared with the Lazuli Glintstone Sword . While this can make attacking into an attacking foe a bit less punishing, the resulting Stamina damage that will be taken if hit can prevent the user from attacking or rolling away. While the brief hit-stun taken by shields isn't suffered when hit, the low Stability means that using the unique heavy attack is highly likely to result in a stun from being guardbroken. Stability can be increased by 10% via two-handing and then further by the Greatshield Talisman .

title: Carian Knight's Sword
text: While the uncharged unique heavy attack deals the same amount of Stance damage as a normal uncharged heavy attack, the charged version only deals 1.5 times more Stance damage instead of the regular straight sword charged version which deals 3 times more Stance damage.

title: Coded Sword
text: Coded Sword — Type: Straight Sword; Attack type: / Standard / Pierce; Skill: Unblockable Blade; FP cost: 18; Weight: 2.5; Sell price: 1000; Tradeable: Yes

title: Coded Sword
text: Coded Sword attribute requirements and scaling — Requires: Faith 20; Scaling: Faith B

title: Coded Sword
text: Coded Sword is a straight sword in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Coded Sword
text: Hidden sword once granted to the Tarnished of the Roundtable by the Two Fingers . A formless cipher comprises its blade, which deals holy damage no shield can repel. Champions would gather at the Roundtable Hold in days long past, when the Two Fingers were masters of oration, their flesh yet full of vigor . Unique Skill: Unblockable Blade Imbue the cipher blade with light, extending its length, then strike with a sudden sweeping attack. This attack cannot be blocked.

title: Coded Sword
text: The Coded Sword can be found on the upper floor of the Fortified Manor in Leyndell, Royal Capital , in the room that mirrors the one that the Two Fingers of the Roundtable Hold can be found it. This weapon cannot be acquired once the Tarnished has defeated Maliketh .

title: Coded Sword
text: As the name suggest, the skill, Unblockable Blade , will pierce through all the damage negation from a blocking shield or weapon. It also has a very wide reach since it's a sweeping attack and deals Stance damage on par with a Straight Sword's charged heavy attack, meaning it can break the Stance of most enemies within 2 hits and most bosses within 3 or 4 hits. Unlike all other weapons, this weapon doesn't bounce away from any shield when wielded one-handed, not even when hitting the Ant's Skull Plate or sturdy enemies such as Miners . Since this weapon has no Physical damage and instead is fully elemental, and its skill fully ignores all the damage negation of a block, it's not very wise to try to block it.

title: Coded Sword
text: Since this weapon deals pure Holy damage it benefits fully from Holy-Shrouding Cracked Tear but is also heavily weakened if the fought enemy has high Holy Damage Negation, something that becomes increasingly common among Bosses after entering Leyndell, Royal Capital , with most of them having 40% Holy damage negation and 3 (technically 2) late-game bosses having 80% Holy damage negation.

title: Crystal Sword
text: Crystal Sword is a straight sword found in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 See Also

title: Crystal Sword
text: Sword fashioned from pure crystal ; a deed impossible for a human . Enwreathed with powerful magic , its attack scales with intelligence . The inscrutable Crystalians have but one clear purpose, to safeguard their crystals unto the end. One theory posits that they yearn for the return of their creator who will carve for them new brethren.

title: Crystal Sword
text: Found after the bridge in the Village of the Albinaurics Site of Grace , located in Liurnia of the Lakes .

title: Crystal Sword
text: The skill, Spinning Slash , is potentially quite damaging, especially if it catches another player offguard and with low Poise , though it's somewhat hampered by the lack of reach on Straight Swords. Does best as a pure Strength weapon, as Dexterity and Intelligence are somewhat hampered by having much lower scaling numbers and being based off the lower Magic base damage respectively. For comparison (unmentioned stats are at the minimum required level): 80 Strength grants 652 Attack Power (AP), 45 Strength and 45 Intelligence grants 632 AP, 45 Strength and 45 Dexterity grants 624 AP, and 80 Intelligence grants 560 AP. A good offhand weapon for those wishing to dual-wield Straight Swords on a Strength build with some Intelligence, due to the ease of upgrading weapons reliant on Somber Smithing Stones and its somewhat mediocre skill not mattering there.

title: Golden Epitaph
text: Golden Epitaph — Type: Straight Sword; Attack type: / Standard / Pierce; Skill: Last Rites; FP cost: 25; Weight: 3.5; Sell price: 500; Tradeable: Yes

title: Golden Epitaph
text: Golden Epitaph attribute requirements and scaling — Requires: Strength 12 (8 two-handed), Dexterity 10, Faith 14; Scaling: Strength D, Dexterity D, Faith D

title: Golden Epitaph
text: Golden Epitaph is a straight sword and melee armament in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 Trivia 6 See Also

title: Golden Epitaph
text: A sword made to commemorate the death of Godwyn the Golden , first of the demigods to die. Infused with the humble prayer of a young boy ; "O brother, lord brother, please die a true death ." Unique Skill : Last Rites Raise the epitaph to grant the effect of Sacred Order to yourself and allies in the vicinity. Particularly effective at laying to rest Those Who Live in Death .

title: Golden Epitaph
text: Found at the entrance of Auriza Hero's Grave . Requires a Stonesword Key to access and is guarded by a Basilisk .

title: Golden Epitaph
text: The skill, Last Rites , functions as an Aura Buff and adds 25 Holy damage and a universal 10% (5% for PvP) damage bonus, as well as a 100% damage bonus against Those Who Live in Death . While being an Aura Buff does mean it will stack with a lot of other buffs, it also places it in competition with Golden Vow (especially the spell version), which also adds a strong boost to damage negation on top of its damage boost, and the Golden Epitaph being Faith -leaning means meetings its requirements are easy. However, Last Rites is generally quicker to use and doesn't require a Sacred Seal to use, making it possibly more convenient in combat. Since the flat damage bonus is applied to all attacks used by the player, this means it's also applied to effects that tick repeatedly or hit several times, such as Volcano Pots and Bestial Sling .

title: Golden Epitaph
text: This weapon has an innate 30% damage bonus against Those Who Live in Death ( Skeletons , Deathbirds and such), on top of dealing the Holy damage that they are usually vulnerable to. Has the most Attack Power (AP) on a hybrid Strength /Faith build or a hybrid Dexterity /Faith build, with a hybrid Strength/Dexterity build being a bit weaker, and a pure Faith build being weaker still. For comparison (unmentioned stats are at the minimum required level): 45 Strength or 45 Dexterity and 45 Faith grants 602 AP, 45 Strength and 45 Dexterity grants 591 AP, and 80 Faith grants 581 AP. The hybrid Strength/Dexterity build has the advantage of not competing with the spell version of Golden Vow and, with 2 more points in Faith, serves as a good offhand weapon to the Miquellan Knight's Sword (though that weapon is acquired much, much later than the Epitaph).

text: Upgraded with Somber Smithing Stones . Cannot have its Affinity and Skill changed. title: Golden Epitaph
text: An epitaph is "a short piece of writing or a poem about a dead person, especially one written on their gravestone" ( Cambridge Dictionary ), which in this case would be the phrase "O brother, lord brother, please die a true death." in the description. This weapons resembles the Rune Sword from Demon's Souls. Patch 1.12 fixed a bug that caused damage to the player and friendly summons when using the "Last Rites" Skill under specific circumstances. Prior to the fix, Last Rites would be applied to non-damaging, persistent effects such as Terra Magica , making them deal the flat 25 Holy damage to any friendly players standing on it. Patch 1.07 increased effect duration, increased attack power, effect against Those Who Live in Death has been revised upward.

title: Golden Epitaph
text: The skill's duration was increased from 45 seconds to 60 seconds, the universal 10% damage buff was added (limited to 5% in PvP), and the damage bonus against Those Who Live in Death was increased from 50% to 100%. Patch 1.04 increased cast speed. For the skill, that is.

title: Lazuli Glintstone Sword
text: A sword with a glintstone in its handguards. Wielded by the scholars of the Lazuli Conspectus who seek to master Carian sorcery . Deals magic damage . Apparently once used as a staff of sorcery , this sword is made out of wood.

title: Lazuli Glintstone Sword
text: Dropped by Lazuli Glintstone Sorcerers with a 2% drop rate. One can be easily reached just outside of the room with the Schoolhouse Classroom Site of Grace, just before the Red Wolf of Radagon in Academy of Raya Lucaria .

title: Lazuli Glintstone Sword
text: The locked, innate skill, while not unique and reliant on its weaker Intelligence -scaling, is very strong for both the projectile bullet part and the weapon stab part. Gains the most Attack Power (AP) from a hybrid Strength /Intelligence build (600 AP from having 45 Strength, 9 Dexterity , and 45 Intelligence), a bit less from a pure Strength build (579 AP from 80 Strength, 9 Dexterity, and 13 Intelligence), and even less from a pure Intelligence build (561 AP from 8 Strength, 12 Dexterity, and 80 Intelligence). Note that since the projectile part of the skill is a bullet-type it scales with associated stat, in this case Intelligence, meaning that the pure Intelligence build will deal the most damage with that part of the skill, and that the pure Strength build will most likely end up dealing somewhat less damage overall with the skill.

title: Lazuli Glintstone Sword
text: Has a unique blocking heavy attack, shared with the Carian Knight's Sword . While this can make attacking into an attacking foe a bit less punishing, the resulting Stamina damage that will be taken if hit can prevent the user from attacking or rolling away. While the brief hit-stun taken by shields isn't suffered when hit, the low Stability means that using the unique heavy attack is highly likely to result in a stun from being guardbroken. Stability can be increased by 10% via two-handing and then further by the Greatshield Talisman . While the uncharged unique heavy attack deals the same amount of Stance damage as a normal uncharged heavy attack, but the charged version only deals 1.5 times more Stance damage, instead of the regular charged version which deals 3 times more Stance damage.

Closed Network Test A straight sword with a double-edged blade. In addition to its easily-inflicted standard damage, this well-balanced weapon can also perform powerful piercing attacks.

title: Longsword
text: Starting armament of the Vagabond origin . Purchased from Twin Maiden Husks at the Roundtable Hold for 1,000 Runes .

title: Longsword
text: The default skill is useful for breaking the guard of blocking enemies with the light attack version or for breaking their Stance with the heavy attack version. Consistently the second strongest of all regular Straight Swords, outmatched only by the Broadsword , almost matching it on a Keen affinity and outclassing it on a Quality affinity. This may be somewhat surprising since the Longsword's scaling values leans towards Strength more so than Dexterity , but it's primarily because the Broadsword scales rather less well on Keen. Quality affinity requires high stats to use properly, making this somewhat difficult to exploit before New Game Plus/Journey 2 and later.

title: Longsword
text: While not quite to the level of the Short Sword's unique heavy attack chain, since the first heavy attack in a chain is a Pierce damage attack as well as the heavy variant of its default skill and as such can hit for heavy counter damage with the Spear Talisman .

title: Longsword
text: Upgraded with regular Smithing Stones . Can have its Affinity and Skill changed. All non-physical Affinities lower all types of guarded damage negation by 5%, except for the associated element where the damage negation increases by 25% and another element also increases by 15%. Magic negation receives the secondary 15% negation bonus on Sacred and Cold affinities, Fire negation gets the increase on Lightning affinity, Lightning negation gets the increase on Fire and Flame Art affinities, and Holy negation gets the increase on Magic affinity. title: Antspur Rapier

Giant Ants deliver stings to grabbed players. Maleigh Marais , a personal believer in the outer god of Rot , obsessed over Malenia, Blade of Miquella .

Unique Skill : Blindfold of Happiness Swing the head while covering the eyes, unleashing a wail of lamentation while temporarily making the user of this skill harder to see. To those who seek happiness, blindness is bliss.

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: Nanaya's Torch is a melee armament in Shadow of the Erdtree . Contents 1 Description 2 Effects 2.1 Skill 3 Acquisition 4 Notes 5 See also

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: A torch made by attaching a dying flame of frenzy to a small spinal column. In a distant land, in an age long past, was born a man who failed to become the Lord of Frenzied Flame . All that remains of him is cradled gently by Nanaya . Unique Skill : Feeble Lord's Flame Hold torch out in front and spit out frenzied flame. Afflicts those caught in its area of effect with madness , along with the user themselves. The skill will continue for as long as the button is held down. Can be used while moving.

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: Deals 55 (134) Strike damage , 99 (242) Fire damage , 12 (35/23) Stance damage and inflicts 65 Madness buildup per hit. Costs 15 (22/14) Stamina per swing.

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: Feeble Lord's Flame deals Fire damage and inflicts Madness buildup in a circular stream or random arc. Medium projectile range Costs 3 FP per tick. Costs 10 Stamina per tick. Deals 1 Stance damage per projectile on hit. Inflicts Madness buildup on wielder and targets hit.

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: Location : Midra's Manse Nanaya's Torch is found in the arms of Nanaya , who sits in a chair on the upper balcony in the western wing of Midra's Manse.

title: Nanaya's Torch
text: Bolstering Material: Somber Smithing Stones Bolstering cost: 270 The spinal column may represent Midra 's flame or that of another, nameless lord.

title: Sentry's Torch
text: Torch given to protectors of the Erdtree . Its flames are bestowed with a special incantation which allows the bearer to see assassins cloaked in veils . Furnished on behalf of the Erdtree and the Grace-Given Lord such that a Night of Black Knives will never come again. Skill : Torch Attack Thrust torch forward to set foes ablaze. This skill is especially effective against foes who are weak to fire .

title: Sentry's Torch
text: Bought from the Hermit Merchant for 7000 runes in Altus Plateau near the first rampart of the capital.

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: St. Trina's Torch is a melee armament in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Effects 3 Acquisition 4 Notes 5 Gallery 6 Trivia 7 See also 8 References

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: Candlestand torch that burns with a light-purple flame . The carving depicts St. Trina , but in adult form, somewhat unnervingly. The light-purple flame induces sleep . Unique Skill : Fires of Slumber Blow into the candlestick's flame, creating a stream of hazy purple fire to cover the ground ahead. The intoxicating flames inflict the sleep ailment upon foes.

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: Causes sleep buildup.

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: Location : Consecrated Snowfield St. Trina's Torch is found inside a wandering Great Coffin caravan in the southeastern Consecrated Snowfield.

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: St. Trina's once-prophesied adult form, stoic and crowned by a flower with eight petals, has an awakened third eye and eight untamed braids of hair—traits associated with outer gods . The torch also depicts the spiral and Trina's Lilies , while a length of rope cord coils around its handle.

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: St. Trina's Torch concept Art Depiction of adult St. Trina

title: St. Trina's Torch
text: The Chinese and Korean version 1.00 release of the game preserve a very different earlier draft of this item description, roughly translating to " Trina's Torch: A torch with an exquisitely designed candlestick. Seeking Death since ancient times, this sacred relic has been entrusted with the mission of crusading against Death. It can illuminate Death's darkness. " [ 1 ]

title: Eleonora's Poleblade
text: Twinned naginata forged in the Land of Reeds . Chosen weapon of Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger . Her mastery of the sword was such that her onslaught was likened to a whirlwind , but now her legacy is stained by accursed blood . Unique Skill : Bloodblade Dance Leap at foe to perform a flurry of tornado-like attacks. Follow up with an additional input to perform an attack that ends in an evasive maneuver.

title: Eleonora's Poleblade
text: Defeating Eleonora, Violet Bloody Finger at the Second Church of Marika in the Altus Plateau .

title: Euporia
text: Euporia is a Twinblade , a melee armament in the Elden Ring add-on Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree .

title: Euporia
text: Twinblade symbolizing abundance. The secret treasure of the tower . Though the blades, fashioned from golden shoots, are largely wilted and darkened, their luster can be restored by dealing damage to foes. However, damage dealt to Those Who Live in Death will have no such effect. Unique Skill : Euporia Vortex Using the power of the vortex, violently twirl the armament, dealing multiple slashes directly ahead. The greater the restored luster of the blades, the greater the power of this skill . However, use of this skill will fully consume the blades' luster.

title: Euporia
text: This armament can be found in Belurat, Tower Settlement . However, reaching it can only be done through Enir-Ilim . On the stairs past the lightning-aspected Divine Beast Warrior , jump off the path onto a lower staircase and then follow the ledges down until you reach a platform with a Fireproof Pickled Liver . Jump into the nearby window (sprinting is advised), and then go out to the balcony and follow the path to the right down the lifts, past the Inquisitors and the Divine Beast Warrior . Euporia lies at the end of this path.

title: Euporia
text: Abundance and Decay Twinblade

text: The Giant's Red Braid is a Whip , a melee armament in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 See also 4 Note 5 See Also

title: Giant's Red Braid
text: Hefty whip woven from the flame -red hair of a Fire Giant . Every giant is red of hair, and Radagon was said to have despised his own red locks. Perhaps that was a curse of their kind. Unique Skill : Flame Dance Imbue the red braid with the Giants' Flame and lash out in a wide range with a series of agile swings.

title: Giant's Red Braid
text: Trade the Remembrance of the Fire Giant , gained by defeating Fire Giant , with Finger Reader Enia . Alternatively, use one of the Wandering Mausoleums to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough using either method).

title: Giant's Red Braid
text: The Japanese description uses "絶望" to describe what Radagon feels about his own red hair, which can be translated as "despaired", rather than "despised".

title: Hoslow's Petal Whip
text: Metal whip formed of razor-sharp chain-link blades that have the appearance of flower petals. This work of art is handed down through the generations of the illustrious House Hoslow . High dexterity is required to wield this weapon, but it excels at inflicting devastating blood loss . "The tale of House Hoslow is told in blood ."

title: Hoslow's Petal Whip
text: Dropped by Juno Hoslow, Knight of Blood after invading him at the Shack of the Lofty on the Mountaintops of the Giants . The invasion sign appears after receiving the Red Letter , the final contract from Tanith, Volcano Manor Proprietress .

title: Magma Whip Candlestick
text: This ritual implement is a three pronged candlestick with solid flames formed of the magma of Mt. Gelmir . When wielded as a weapon, the flames become supple whips of lava. Unique Skill : Sea of Magma Swing a molten whip overhead to temporarily cover the surrounding area in a sea of magma. Hold to continue swinging the lava whip.

title: Magma Whip Candlestick
text: Reward from Patches in Volcano Manor after defeating Great Horned Tragoth . Won't be given the first time he is spoken too. Instead, the area has to be reloaded in some way (either via teleporting or by exiting and re-entering the game) and he can be spoken to again to gain the weapon.

title: Tooth Whip
text: Whip bestrewn with rotting, misshapen teeth. Filthy and seething with disease, the teeth are embedded in the whip and dose the victim with deadly poison upon each strike . As the wounds ripen they grow inflamed and ooze pus. The flesh of shamans was said to meld harmoniously with others. Unique Skill : Painful Strike A chastening whip strike, honed to maximize pain. Temporarily reduces the rate at which stamina recovers. Thus does the pain encourage obedience.

title: Lion Greatbow
text: The Lion Greatbow is a Greatbow , a ranged armament in Elden Ring . It fires equipped Great Arrows . It is General Radahn 's secondary weapon. Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Trivia 5 Upgrading 6 See Also

title: Lion Greatbow
text: Greatbow of black iron wielded by General Radahn . Decorated with a lion motif. Imbued with gravitational power of the Starscourge, when used along with Radahn's Spear , it becomes a true weapon of a champion . Unique Skill : Radahn's Rain Archery skill performed from a low stance. Ready the bow and fire a sudden flurry of arrows up into the sky. The arrows will pour on foes like rain.

title: Lion Greatbow
text: Trade the Remembrance of the Starscourge , gained by defeating Starscourge Radahn , with Finger Reader Enia . Alternatively, use one of the Wandering Mausoleums with a bell to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough either way).

title: Lion Greatbow
text: Its Strength requirement is effectively always 15 as Greatbows can only be wielded two-handed. Boosts the damage of Radahn's Spears by 20% when held in an active hand. Note that this remains true even if it isn't used to shoot them, as long as this weapon is in the other hand. Using two separate Lion Greatbows grants the boost twice, for a 44% damage increase. Scales slightly better with Strength than with Dexterity , though a hybrid Strength/Dexterity build fares a bit better than a pure Strength build. A 45/45 split of the Attributes gives 501 Attack Rating (AR). An 80/18 (minimum Dexterity) split gives 488 AR (effectively 120 Strength due to the 50% boost from two-handing). A 54/18 (minimum Dexterity) split gives 470 AR (effectively 81 Strength due to the 50% boost from two-handing). A 15/80 (minimum Strength) split gives 466 AR.

title: Lion Greatbow
text: Patch 1.09 increased this weapon's scaling values.

title: Lion Greatbow
text: Upgraded with Somber Smithing Stones . Can't be buffed or be infused with Ashes of War .

title: Lion Greatbow

title: Bone Bow
text: A crude shortbow fashioned from sickly bone. A medium for spirit -calling, and a product of the ancient hexing arts of the tower . Unique Skill : Rancor Shot Imbue arrows with vengeful spirits , before firing off a barrage. Imbued arrows chase down foes as they cut through the air.

title: Bone Bow
text: The Bone Bow uses Rancor Shot , which causes fired arrows to deal extra Magic damage and aggressively track their targets. Additionally, arrows may be fired more quickly. Rapid-fire homing projectiles Costs 3 FP per projectile Costs 9 Stamina per projectile Deals 1.6 stance damage per projectile

title: Bone Bow
text: Location : Belurat, Tower Settlement The Bone Bow is found on a corpse in the Upper Residential Ward of Belurat, Tower Settlement. From the Belurat, Tower Settlement site of grace, turn left and ascend the elevator shortcut, then turn left again to enter the bottom of the spiral staircase tower. The bow is on the other side of the room, guarded by numerous Baby Spider Scorpions and two Large Spider Scorpions .

Drop onto the east balcony and enter the torch-lit, open doorway to find the item. Beware the Omenkiller .

title: Azur's Glintstone Staff
text: Staff of the primeval glintstone sorcerer Azur . Only those who have glimpsed what lies beyond the wisdom of stone may wield it. Reduces casting time of sorceries , but consumes additional FP .

title: Azur's Glintstone Staff
text: Found in a secluded room on the highest level of the Church of the Cuckoo . The level can be accessed via the rooftops of the Academy of Raya Lucaria . From the Debate Parlor site of grace, go outside to the left until you meet two Raya Lucaria Sorcerers . Jump down on the stairs next to them. Continue forward until you can jump onto the rooftops. Then, proceed further and go up the ladder at the end of said rooftops. After the sorcerer, go down on the rooftops again, until you can see a dome-like structure that has windows. Go through one of the windows, and descend onto the lower floor. One of the exits leads to a enclosed bridge, with a High Page guarding the secluded room. The staff is in the room behind him, in front of the inactive School of Graven Mages .

title: Azur's Glintstone Staff
text: Increases FP cost of Sorceries by 20%, in exchange for speeding up their casting time even more than the Radagon Icon . Casting speed is decreased for any spell cast when this staff is held in one of a character's two active hands, even if this staff isn't used to cast it. Adds 40 "virtual" Dexterity for the purpose of improving casting speed. The casting speed decrease applies to both Incantations and Sorceries but the FP cost increase only applies to Sorceries. This means that Incantation users can keep this Staff in the off-hand with no penalty aside from its weight and taking up an active hand-slot. A +10 Azur's Glintstone Staff gains 254 Sorcery Scaling with minimum stats (52 Intelligence ), 279 Sorcery Scaling at 60 Intelligence, 320 Sorcery Scaling at 70 Intelligence, and 362 Sorcery Scaling at 80 Intelligence.

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: The Dragon Communion Seal is a Sacred Seal in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Upgrading 5 Notes 6 Trivia 7 See Also

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: Formless drakeblood seal with a dragon communion crest. Enhances Dragon Communion incantations . The sacrificial devouring of the heart gives power. Indeed, Dragon Communion is too primal in nature for the term "incantation" to be appropriate. This seal scales incantations with one's arcane attribute.

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: Dropped by a spectral Banished Knight located in the Fringefolk Hero's Grave in Limgrave . To find it, start at the Stranded Graveyard Site of Grace and head down the first two ramps in the Grave, then head right in the split to go up another two ramps. The Knight is inside a small room at the end of the ramp and is quite tough for the early game. The Chariot in the dungeon can be used to help kill it, though that comes at considerable risk to the player as well.

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: This Sacred Seal is best used for split Arcane / Faith builds, with an even split being slightly more favorable over one more biased towards Arcane. It can also be used for a pure Arcane build but that alternative is slightly weaker, by about 7-8%. For comparison: a 45/45 Arc/Fth split gives 336 Scaling at +10, a 60/30 Arc/Fth split gives 333 Scaling at +10, and a 80/10 Arc/Fth split gives 310 Scaling at +10. Holding a Dragon Communion Seal (DCS) in one of your active hands will boost the damage of directly damaging Dragon Communion Incantations by 15%. Holding a DCS in each active hand (that is, two Seals that are each held in separate hands) will increase the damage boost to 32.25% (1.15*1.15=1.3225). The damage boost is still granted if the other piece of equipment is two-handed. This bonus stacks with the 20% damage boost granted when reactivating the Rock Heart .

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: Any incantation that inflicts Poison (except for Poison Armament ), Madness , or non-bloodflame Blood Loss build-up and is cast using this Seal will have their Status Effect scale with the wielder's Arcane stat. This status build-up bonus is equivalent to a fifth of the regular Weapon Scaling bonus from Stat Scaling, meaning that with a +10 DCS's scaling with Arcane being at 2.25 this caps at 45% (0.2*2.25=0.45). Of the Blood Loss-inflicting incantations only Swarm of Flies inflicts any direct Blood Loss-build up (70 build-up out of its total 150 build-up) as direct status build-up instead of as part of a non-scaling bloodflame effect. It would theoretically also scale the amount of Sleep build-up of any incantation that inflicts such a Status Effect. However, no incantation that inflicts that status effect exists.

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: Curiously Scarlet Rot build-up doesn't scale with Arcane , even though the spell type can inflict that status effect. The weight being 0 means and the existence of Physical Dragon Communion Incantations such as Dragonmaw and Rotten Breath means this Seal works well with the Blue Dancer Charm weight-based increase to Physical damage. This synergy is further enhanced by the existence of the Rock Heart , essentially a 0 weight armor set that can increase the damage of Dragon Communion Incantations even further. This Seal is very unsuitable for casting Weapon Buff or direct healing spells as those depend entirely on the Faith portion of the used Seal's scaling, and this Seal only have about a third of the Faith scaling that pure Faith scaling Seals such as the Finger Seal and Erdtree Seal have.

title: Dragon Communion Seal
text: Patch 1.04 decreased the scaling of status effect build-up from spells and incantations of Albinauric Staff and Dragon Communion Seal. Previously both of these casting implements full Arcane -scaling would apply to the Status Effects build-up of spells cast with them. For a +10 Dragon Communion Seal, this meant that almost the full S (2.25) would be applied thus resulting in roughly 3 times the Status Effect build-up on builds with high Arcane which would often result in instant or near instant application of Madness with spells such as Frenzied Burst .

title: Erdtree Seal
text: The Erdtree Seal is the strongest Faith -only Sacred Seal when the stat is above 68. Gets 353 Incantation Scaling at 80 Faith when the seal is at +10. The other seals are in the 310 to 330 range at that point. If using only a single Seal, other Faith-only Seals will still outmatch the Erdtree Seal when using the type of incantations that they boost. However, since this boost also applies to other Seals when a spell-boosting seal is held in an active hand, wielding two Seals at the same time makes the Erdtree Seal once again the stronger alternative unless two copies of the same spell-boosting seal are obtained.

title: Erdtree Seal
text: Though it won't quite catch up with the Godslayer's Seal (the strongest Seal beforehand) until the Faith stat reaches the 60s, the ease of upgrading it because it requires Somber Smithing Stones (versus the unfortunately generally much rarer Smithing Stones that the Godslayer's Seal needs) means it can still see good use beforehand. Besides increasing the Seal's Incantation Scaling , the Faith-scaling also increases the its own meager base Physical and Holy damage.

title: Erdtree Seal
text: The Erdtree crest mentioned in the description is the one temporarily created on the ground or in the air when casting Erdtree Incantations . This is the only Sacred Seal that also deals non- Physical damage when it's used to directly hit an enemy.

text: A sacred seal bestowed by a Finger Maiden which serves as a catalyst for casting sacred incantations . Incantations must be memorized first at a site of lost grace , and a sacred seal must be equipped to cast them.

title: Finger Seal
text: Can be purchased from the Twin Maiden Husks for 800 Runes . Obtained by default if selecting the Confessor or Prophet origins.

title: Finger Seal
text: Used to cast Incantations . No bonus to any spells, including Two Fingers Incantations .

title: Finger Seal
text: At 249% Faith scaling this Seal is technically the third strongest in the game, and the strongest if your Faith is between 10 and 26. Do note that it is outclassed by any Sacred Seal with a bonus to certain spells if those spells are being cast. At 10 Faith it has 126 Incantation Scaling . At 20 Faith it has 156 Incantation Scaling. At 30 Faith it has 188 Incantation Scaling. At 40 Faith it has 221 Incantation Scaling. At 50 Faith it has 254 Incantation Scaling. At 60 Faith it has 286 Incantation Scaling. At 70 Faith it has 305 Incantation Scaling. At 80 Faith it has 329 Incantation Scaling. Consistently 3-5 points behind the Godslayer's Seal after reaching 27 Faith and above, making it quite viable to keep the Finger Seal upgraded for regular use if you have already invested in it and are somewhat lacking in regular Smithing Stones.

title: Finger Seal
text: Actually stronger than the Erdtree Seal until 66 Faith, and thereafter it becomes outclassed, which in combination with the latter requiring the much more common Somber Smithing Stones makes the latter option much more favorable. No bonus to any incantations, not even the ones granted by the Two Fingers , a trait shared only with the Erdtree Seal among the Sacred Seals

title: Finger Seal
text: Upgraded with regular Smithing Stones .

title: Bosses
text: | For Elden Ring: Nightreign, seek Nightreign Bosses . Bosses are powerful enemies that can be encountered in Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree . Contents 1 Overview 1.1 Mandatory bosses 1.2 Shardbearer bosses 1.3 Remembrance bosses 1.4 Achievement bosses 2 List of bosses in Elden Ring 3 List of bosses in Shadow of the Erdtree 4 List of Unmarked Bosses 5 Notes 6 See also 7 References

title: Bosses
text: Bosses can be found roaming the overworld or inside one of the many locations in the Lands Between . There are in total 238 bosses. Several key indicators can identify a boss fight. Firstly, the music changes to a more intense or dramatic score. A health bar and the boss's name appear prominently at the bottom of the screen. Bosses yield a larger amount of Runes when defeated, and their items rewards are added directly to the inventory. Unlike common enemies, bosses are not revived when the player rests at a Site of Grace . Some bosses appear out in the open world and will attack the player if they wander too close. These field bosses may be disengaged and fled from. The player can lead them a short distance away from their spawn points. Certain field bosses only appear at night: Deathbirds , Death Rite Birds , and Night's Cavalry .

title: Bosses
text: If the player challenges the dungeon boss, they are trapped within the room by a fog wall until the boss is defeated. If the player is defeated, they may be required to pass through a fog gate to rechallenge the boss. Some difficult bosses may be encountered early on, which can drive player exploration.

title: Bosses
text: Over two hundred bosses are available to fight, but only a few must be beaten to finish the game. These bosses are encountered after acquiring two Great Runes and gaining access to Leyndell, Royal Capital . The boss fights that are not optional and must be beaten to progress include: Any two shardbearers: Godrick , Mohg , Rennala , Rykard , or Radahn Draconic Tree Sentinel or Fia's Champions Godfrey, First Elden Lord Morgott, the Omen King Fire Giant Godskin Duo Maliketh, the Black Blade Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing Godfrey, First Elden Lord Radagon of the Golden Order Elden Beast Two bosses must be defeated in order to access the Shadow of the Erdtree : Starscourge Radahn Mohg, Lord of Blood Similarly, the bosses mandatory for completing the Shadow of the Erdtree are: Messmer the Impaler Romina, Saint of the Bud Promised Consort Radahn

title: Bosses
text: Main article : Shardbearers Two or more Great Runes are required to break a seal and enter Leyndell, Royal Capital . Two Shardbearers must be defeated to complete the story: Godrick the Grafted Mohg, Lord of Blood Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy Starscourge Radahn

title: Bosses
text: Main article : Remembrance Bosses Defeating a Remembrance Boss grants their corresponding Remembrance , which can be traded to Finger Reader Enia for one of two unique items . Additionally, the Remembrance becomes available for duplication at any Wandering Mausoleum or Remembrance Duplication Coffin .

title: Bosses
text: A victory banner is displayed whenever a boss is defeated. Its message reflects the power of the defeated adversary. The tiered categories are: Enemy, Great Enemy, Legend, Demigod, and God. Bosses block both routes to Leyndell, Royal Capital . Route A : Defeat any two Shardbearers and then Draconic Tree Sentinel . Route B : Defeat any one Shardbearer boss, Starscourge Radahn , Mimic Tear , Valiant Gargoyles , and then Fia's Champions .

title: Abductor Virgin
text: Abductor Virgins , also known as Iron Virgins , are Bosses and Enemies in Elden Ring . They are Bosses in Elden Ring Nightreign . Contents 1 Overview 2 Drops 3 Bosses 4 Notes 5 Gallery 6 References

title: Abductor Virgin
text: Abductor Virgins are automatons affiliated with the Volcano Manor . Originally designed with the intent of capturing and transposing victims for interrogation, they can be found strewn around the Lands Between . Though the majority have lost their powers of transposition, they will still attempt to fulfill their original directive if approached. To aid in their task, Abductor Virgins have been outfitted with heavy weaponry on both arms that alter how they approach each target. When a Virgin has approached a target, they will open their chest cavity and attempt to pull them in with serpentine tendrils. Given the loss of their transposition ability, the target will be constricted instead. If they survive this, they will be spat back out.

title: Abductor Virgin
text: They are weak to Lightning damage and all damage taken is greatly increased if attacked when their chest cavity is opened during a charge or attempted grab.

title: Abductor Virgin
text: This enemy has no drops.

The latter will make itself known immediately at the start of the fight. Whereas the former will charge at the player from the left side of the cave.

title: Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader
text: Alecto was the ringleader of the Black Knife Assassins , the assailants responsible for the murder of Godwyn the Golden on the Night of Black Knives . [ 1 ] She was believed to be a Numen originating from an Eternal City . [ 2 ] Alecto was said to have had close ties to Queen Marika herself. [ 3 ]

title: Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader
text: After stealing a fragment of Destined Death , Ranni the Witch performed a dark ritual to imbue its power into mishappen blades. [ 4 ] [ 5 ] Alecto and her fellow assassins took the daggers, donned silent, silver scale armor, and made their way toward the Royal Capital . [ 6 ] [ 3 ] [ 2 ] Alecto masked her presence with a concealing veil as she and her co-conspirators entered Leyndell beneath the cover of darkness. [ 7 ] When the moment was right, they carved the Cursemark of Death into Godwyn's flesh and slew his soul. Alecto's daughter, Tiche , died protecting her during their ensuing flight from the capital. [ 1 ] Alecto herself was later captured and sealed within an Evergaol by an unknown party. [ 8 ]

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: | For the other boss, seek Regal Ancestor Spirit . A number of new growths bud from the antler-like horns of the fallen king, each glowing with light. Thus does new life grow from death, and from death, one obtains power. - Ancestral Spirit's Horn The Ancestor Spirit ( 祖霊 , Sorei , Ancestral Spirit) is an optional boss in Elden Ring . It also appears as a field boss in Elden Ring Nightreign . Contents 1 Background 2 Overview 3 Drops 4 Location 5 Stats 5.1 Resistances 6 Moveset 7 Notes 8 Trivia 9 Gallery 10 See Also 11 External Links 12 References

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: The antler-like horns of a long-lived stag continued budding long after death, over and over again, until the beast became an Ancestor Spirit. Ancestral Spirits are a phenomenon that exists beyond the purview of the Erdtree , following the principle that life sprouts from death, just as it does birth, granting power. Its horned remains are found at the southern Hallowhorn Grounds on the bank of the Siofra River , where it lies beneath the false night sky. Its large, antler-like horns sprouted small leafy plants that shone with light, infusing nearby Ancestral Followers with profound spirituality. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ]

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: This boss is optional. Furled Fingers or Spirit Ashes can be summoned for this boss. Deals Standard and Magic damage

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: 13,000 runes Ancestral Follower Ashes

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: The horned remains of this Ancestor Spirit are found at the Hallowhorn Grounds in the lower section of the Siofra River , accessible via the Siofra River Well in Limgrave . This ruined structure is situated southeast of the Siofra River Bank Site of Grace . In order to face this boss, the player must first light eight beacons scattered throughout the area. Each beacon corresponds to a pillar flanking the staircase to the Hallowhorn Grounds. Once all signals are lit, the following message displays: Power gathers somewhere in horned remains . The horned remains will burned with blue spectral flames, and agreeing to Touch horned remains will grant a supernatural encounter with the spirit.

title: Ancient Dragon Lansseax
text: Lansseax, sister of Fortissax , was an Ancient Dragon that took the form of a human priestess to commune with the knights of Leyndell 's Ancient Dragon Cult . [ 1 ] Her most beloved knight was Vyke the Dragonspear , [ 2 ] a Tarnished knight of the Roundtable Hold , who was later scorched by the Frenzied Flame . [ 3 ]

title: Ancient Dragon Lansseax
text: Lansseax is first encountered near the Abandoned Coffin Site of Grace , ambushing the player near the entrance to Altus Plateau from the Ruin-Strewn Precipice . After sustaining enough damage, she abruptly ends the fight by flying away. Her next encounter is on a hill southwest of the Rampartside Path Site of Grace, located in eastern Altus Plateau, where she can be defeated for good.

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: Formerly a Dragon Communion warrior, the Ancient Dragon-Man was once arbiter of those worthy to devour the Dreaded One. - Dragon-Hunter's Great Katana Ancient Dragon-Man is an Invader and optional Enemy boss in Shadow of the Erdtree . Contents 1 Overview 2 Moveset 3 Notes 3.1 Strategy 4 Trivia 5 Gallery 6 See also 7 References

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: The Ancient Dragon-Man is enountered twice in the Gravesite Plain —once as an Invader and once as a formal boss. His Invasion triggers on the road southwest of the Pillar Path Waypoint Site of Grace . He is fought up the hill past Igon , who audibly cowers on the ground. The boss version is encountered at the bottom of the Dragon's Pit. Defeating him opens the path to the Foot of the Jagged Peak , which connects to Charo's Hidden Grave and the Jagged Peak . Formerly a drake warrior who hunted colossal dragons , the Ancient Dragon-Man partook in the original form of Dragon Communion by consuming a jagged Rock Heart . [ 1 ] He took on the stony form of a small, bipedal ancient dragon and became the arbiter of those worthy to devour Bayle the Dread . [ 2 ]

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: Wields the Dragon-Hunter's Great Katana and Dragon Communion Seal . Uses the Magma Breath and Dragonmaw Incantations . Prefers two-handed jumping attacks and Dragonwound Slash . Has transformed using a Rock Heart .

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: Ancient Dragon-Man has a single Flask of Crimson Tears . He has 0 Poise and 0% Damage Negation for each Damage Type . Draconic Tree Sentinels also became dragons to better fight dragons. [ 3 ]

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: It is possible to defeat the Ancient Dragon-Man without having leveled up or upgraded one's Weapons , Flasks and even Scadutree Blessings with relative comfort through the use of spamming the crouching light attack from Whips . As its long reach, fast attack-speed, low stamina-consumption and early stamina-regeneration, combined with the Ancient Dragon-Man's non-existent poise, allows the player to consistently harass him from a relatively safe distance without needing to worry about him trying to excessively dodge and then rush the player down like conventional projectiles from spells or bows would. The player can increase their un-upgraded weapon's damage-output through the use of Blood Loss , Frostbite and Poison procs that are then immediately followed by the use of the Skill , The Poison Flower Blooms Twice as a third source of blood loss-like burst-damage.

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: The most efficient set-up for this would be to have two different whips they can alternate between. Hoslow's Petal Whip and Thorned Whip are excellent choices as they can both have their Affinities changed through the use of Ashes of War , while at the same time preserving their innate blood loss buildup no matter what affinity is chosen. So one Poison whip with Ash of War: The Poison Flower Blooms Twice and a Cold whip with almost any ash of the user's choosing.

title: Ancient Dragon-Man

title: Ancient Hero of Zamor
text: Main article : Zamor Knight The three ancient heroes of Zamor earned renown during the War Against the Giants , where they finally triumphed over their mortal enemies. [ 1 ] They hail from the Town of Zamor , and cloak themselves in icy tempests. [ 2 ] They appear as tall, slender humanoids with long, silver hair and ornate masks.

title: Arbor Sentinel
text: Silver helm of the Arbor Sentinels who serve the sacred tree of Miquella, the Scion Empyrean. Features a huge circular wood crest. Hailing from Raya Lucaria, these enchanted knights once belonged to the Carian Royal Family , but were later gifted to Miquella, recipient of the Vision, by King Consort Radagon . - Royal Knight Helm Arbor Sentinels was a order of former Carian Knights.

title: Arbor Sentinel
text: Arbor Sentinels, according to promise of Lord Radagon, went to the Haligtree, where they begin a long serve to Miquella.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Far beyond the Lands Between , within the Greater Will 's lightless abyss , the many-colored debris of ancient stars coalesced into a malformed creature. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] A falling star of ill omen, Astel arrived long ago. [ 4 ] It assailed the Eternal Cities with a barrage of meteors, [ 5 ] [ 6 ] [ 7 ] sealing them deep underground, away from the true night sky. [ 8 ] It destroyed the Nameless Eternal City and Noklateo, the Shrouded City . [ 9 ] [ 10 ] [ 11 ]

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: A procession of multi-colored orbs, Astel is an aberrant, cosmic entity that resembles a Great Dragonfly . [ 12 ] It has a humanoid skull with large mandibles and a large blue eye emerging from a crack in its forehead. It has four translucent insect wings and six skeletal arms that end in human-like hands, the forelimbs each possessing six fingers . Its long tail ends in a spiny scorpion stinger, wielded as a flail . It also casts gravity -based magic and can warp to other parts of the arena.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void, is encountered within a secluded, starlit cavern somewhere along the underground Ainsel River , beyond the Lake of Rot . This area is accessed by entering the Transportation Coffin overlooking a chasm at the Grand Cloister . The elevator Astel blocks is the only path to the hidden Moonlight Altar , a necessary step for Ranni the Witch 's questline.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Defeating Astel, Naturalborn of the Void for the first time unlocks the " Astel, Naturalborn of the Void " achievement. Defeating this boss is optional, though it is mandatory for the " Age of the Stars " ending. Astel resembles a Great Dragonfly . Astel, Naturalborn of the Void, is the boss of the Grand Cloister, an area populated by insect-like Kindred of Rot .

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: This boss was named Astel, Misbegotten of the Void in version 1.00 . At the time, the Misbegotten were referred to as "Aberrations." Astel has an unused animation in which it fell from the sky, crashing to the earth like a meteor. This move is used by Starscourge Radahn . The internal data Astel's boss arena includes spawn markers for an Ancestor Spirit and its spirit animals, suggesting the Grand Cloister boss was reassigned to Astel from a third Ancestor Spirit encounter. It is also possible that one of the Ancestor Spirit boss rooms was duplicated and used as a template.

title: Battlemage
text: Battlemage is an enemy in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Overview 1.1 Moveset 2 Drops 3 Bosses 4 References

title: Battlemage
text: Scholars of the Haima Conspectus of the Academy of Raya Lucaria . Haima scholars are the adjudicators of the Academy, [ 1 ] who studied sorceries of cannon and gavel in order to gain the power to quell conflict. [ 2 ] Whenever war broke out and the Academy sealed its gates, the battlemages of Haima were the only scholars who dared venture outside. [ 3 ] As such, they can be found in many regions of the Lands Between . The stone clubs they carry reflect their more forceful approach, allowing them to feel the impact of their blows so they do not lose sight of the true essence of battle.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Curse you, Bayle! I hereby vow you will rue this day! Behold, a true drake warrior and I, Igon! Your fears made flesh! Solid of scale you might be, foul dragon, but I will riddle with holes your rotten hide! With a hail of harpoons! With every last drop of my being! Bayle the Dread…you shall haunt me no longer! - [[ Igon ]] Bayle the Dread is an optional boss in Shadow of the Erdtree . Contents 1 Overview 1.1 Enemy of the Dragonlord 1.2 Dragon Communion 1.3 Drake Warrior 2 Boss Fight 2.1 Phase One 2.2 Phase Two 3 Notes 4 Trivia 5 Gallery 6 See Also 6.1 External Links 7 References

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle the Dread was a legendary black dragon who is said to be the ancestor of the drakes or lesser dragons. Long ago, Bayle betrayed the Ancient dragons and clashed with Dragonlord Placidusax . [ 1 ] Their vicious battle ended in stalemate and left both dragons with grievous injuries and loss of body parts, with Bayle losing his two winged arms, his pair of wings on his back and his left leg, with the dreaded dragon fleeing to the Jagged Peak soon afterwards. [ 2 ] [ 3 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: As punishment for his betrayal, Placidusax created the art of Dragon Communion , sending his followers to shepherd humanity into hunting Bayle and his descendants. [ 4 ] As reward for felling the drakes, the dragon hunters consumed their hearts in order to gain their power.  Bayle was pursued by Florissax , a servant of Placidusax who sought to recruit mortals in the hunt for Bayle. [ 5 ] [ 6 ] Dragonslayer Igon , a particularly obsessive drake warrior, was one such mortal. Igon faced Bayle, but was left broken in both body and spirit by their battle. [ 7 ] [ 8 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: The gravelstone heart of Bayle Igon was stumbled upon by a Tarnished champion , and begged them to summon his spirit to face Bayle once more. Together, the pair succeed in slaying Bayle. [ 9 ] [ 10 ] If Bayle's heart is eaten at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion , his willpower will one day consume the body and soul of the drake warrior. [ 11 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle is weakest against piercing damage, Dragon Communion Grease , and Igon's Harpoons .

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle is stuck on the ground. He commands a unique power that combines fire and lightning damage. He is a

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle summons an extra set of wings made from red lightning. He is able to fly.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Two of Placidusax's lost heads can be found still clinging onto the upside of Bayle's neck. The ancient dragons who sided with Bayle against Placidusax wielded the branching red lightning . [ 12 ] Drake Warrior Igon may be summoned inside the area if the player has received his Furled Finger . Igon and Florissax will reward the player after Bayle is defeated. Bayle is the only boss in the game who displays the text 'Legend Felled' when defeated who does not drop a Remembrance . Rather, he drops the Heart of Bayle , which functions similarly, with some differences. The Heart of Bayle cannot be redeemed for an item by meeting with Finger Reader Enia . Rather, it must be redeemed at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion . The Heart cannot be consumed to receive Runes, nor duplicated.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle's name is a corruption of "bale," which refers to a destructive and evil force. Bayle's name and moniker "The Dread" may also be a reference to the dragon Balerion , The Black Dread from author and the base game's co-writer George R. R. Martin's fantasy novel series, A Song of Ice and Fire .

title: Bayle the Dread

title: Beastman of Farum Azula
text: | For the faction, seek Azula Beastmen . For the skeletons, seek Beast Skeleton . Beastman of Farum Azula are enemies and bosses in Elden Ring and Elden Ring Nightreign . Contents 1 Overview 2 Variants 3 Bosses 4 Gallery 5 References

title: Beastman of Farum Azula
text: Main article : Azula Beastmen The Azula beastmen are anthropomorphic, bestial creatures who reside in Crumbling Farum Azula . They fight using heavy, curved weapons forged from dull iron [ 1 ] , and some use shields made from earthenware jars, which are created to make shields. [ 2 ] The crumbling ruins of Farum Azula are said to be the remains of a giant mausoleum enshrining an Ancient Dragon , and certain Beastmen, who wield weapons clad in lightning, are chosen to guard it. [ 3 ]

title: Beastman of Farum Azula
text: Beastman of Farum Azula Armored Beastman of Farum Azula Variant Description Lightly-armored Beastmen clad in ornate golden jewellery. Patches of their flesh are missing, exposing parts of their bones. They can be found wielding curved swords, jar-shields, or sickle-like throwing blades. Grey-haired Beastmen wield Clawmark Seals , which they use to cast lightning incantations. Some beastmen wield weapons imbued with red lightning, which deal lightning damage. Moveset Drops Item Drop Rate Notes Beastman's Curved Sword 2% Dropped by those who wield it. Beastman's Jar-Shield 2% Dropped by those who wield it. Sanctuary Stone 8% - Gravel Stone 3% Dropped by those who wield Clawmark Seals and red lightning incantations.

text: Black Blade Kindred is a reoccurring boss in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Overview 2 Bosses 3 Gallery 4 References

title: Black Blade Kindred
text: The Black Blade Kindred are Valiant Gargoyles who serve Maliketh, the Black Blade . [ 1 ] These golems wield bronze weapons mended with blackened corpse wax , and are able to harness the power of the Rune of Death , with attacks that drain one's health .

title: Black Blade Kindred
text: Bestial Sanctum Forbidden Lands Background A Black Blade Kindred who stands guard outside the Bestial Sanctum in Caelid . It wields a halberd and a greatsword. It is possible to avoid this boss by using the sending gate north of the Third Church of Marika , which will teleport the player to the door to the Bestial Sanctum, behind the boss.

The assassins that carried out the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives were all women, and rumored to be Numen who had close ties with Marika herself. - Black Knife Armor Black Knife Assassins ( 黒き刃の刺客 , Kuroki Yaiba no Shikaku ) are Bosses and Enemies in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Background 2 Drops 3 Locations 4 Overview 4.1 Resistances 4.2 Moveset 5 Bosses 6 Notes 7 Trivia 8 Gallery 8.1 Videos 9 External Links 10 References

title: Black Knife Assassin
text: Scions of the Eternal Cities , the Black Knife Assassins were a group of women with close ties to Queen Marika the Eternal . [ 1 ] Led by Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader , they were coconspirators of Ranni the Witch , who robbed the Rune of Death from Maliketh, the Black Blade . [ 2 ] She imbued it into Blades of Calling , fashioning a set of god -slaying Black Knives . [ 3 ] [ 4 ] The Assassins carried out the Night of Black Knives , carving Cursemarks of Death into Ranni and Godwyn the Golden . [ 5 ] They wore silent, silver scale armor and used Concealing Veils to mask their presence, rendering them effectively invisible. [ 6 ] [ 7 ] [ 8 ] Presently, they are scattered throughout the Lands Between , and can be found in many different regions .

title: Black Knife Assassin
text: Black Knife Assassin enemies are unmarked bosses that do not respawn once defeated. Their bodies are invisible. They float when they backstep, as if manipulated by unseen strings. Melina uses the Black Knife Assassin model and animations, noticeable when summoned as a cooperator against Morgott, the Omen King . She wields the Blade of Calling , and may cast Minor Erdtree and Miranda's Prayer . Black Knife Assassins have immunity to all Status Effects except Frostbite , corroborating Destined Death's vulnerability to the Eclipse . [ 10 ]

title: Black Knight Edredd
text: Edredd was a member of the Black Knights who crusaded with Messmer the Impaler in the Shadow Realm . [ 1 ] The Black Knights were notorious for their unwavering conviction to endless slaughter in a war without honor. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Edredd never received the Grace of Gold . [ 4 ]

title: Black Knight Edredd

text: Garrew was a member of the Black Knights who crusaded with Messmer the Impaler in the Shadow Realm . [ 1 ] The Black Knights were notorious for their unwavering conviction to endless slaughter in a war without honor. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Garrew never received the Grace of Gold . [ 4 ]

title: Black Knight Garrew
text: A sturdy knight with a Black Steel Greathammer and Greatshield wearing black iron armor accented with gold detailing. [ 1 ] The armor bears the crest of the Scadutree engraved in gold. [ 4 ]

title: Bloodhound Knight
text: | For the boss at Forlorn Hound Evergaol, seek Bloodhound Knight Darriwil . The Bloodhound Knights are trained as hunters and known to be unshakable trackers. Without the use of language, each knight chooses his own master. Once the decision has been made, the knight stays loyal for life. - Bloodhound Knight Armor Bloodhound Knights are enemies and bosses in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Overview 2 Locations 3 Drops 4 Moveset 5 Bosses 6 Notes 7 Gallery 8 See Also 8.1 External Links 9 References

title: Bloodhound Knight
text: Bloodhound Knights are trained as hunters and are known to be unshakable trackers. Without language, they choose their own master whom they will stay loyal to for the rest of their life. [ 1 ] They wield a Bloodhound's Fang alongside a Bloodhound Claw that allows them to slip past an enemy's guard. They use the Ash of War: Bloodhound's Step to either move out of harm's way or to close the gap between them and their opponent quickly. Lesser variants can be found as non-respawning enemies guarding corpses in the overworld.

title: Bloodhound Knight
text: One is found atop a wall near the gate past the Isolated Merchant at Academy of Raya Lucaria , another is in one of the rooms past the illusory wall in Volcano Manor, a third is guarding Eternal Darkness in Swamp Lookout Tower atop the gate wall near Sellia, Town of Sorcery , and a fourth is guarding the Gelmir Knight Set inside of Gelmir Hero's Grave in a room reached by dropping onto rafters below the Chariot 's path.

title: Bloodhound Knight Darriwil
text: A traitorous Bloodhound Knight hiding within an evergaol cell, and is sought out by Blaidd the Half-Wolf . [ 1 ] Summoning Blaidd the Half-Wolf (only possible after having used the Finger Snap gesture to speak with him at Mistwood Ruins ) and letting him take on the brunt of the boss's attacks, while occasionally sneaking in a jumping attack, is a very efficient way to kill this knight.

title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: Called into service when the Queen invoked an oath they swore, the trolls are treated as true knights of Caria, and fight arm in arm with their human comrades. - Troll Knight's Sword Bols, Carian Knight is an optional boss in Elden Ring . title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: A hulking troll knight clad in heavy armor and wielding a massive greatsword, Bols was one of the Trolls called into service by the Carians , fighting as a true enchanted knight alongside human comrades. [ 1 ] Bols was imprisoned within the Cuckoo's Evergaol in western Liurnia of the Lakes by the Knights of the Cuckoo during the Liurnian Civil War .

title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: A towering troll knight with slow, sweeping greatsword attacks that create shockwaves. His size makes it easy to pass between his legs and circle to his back. He can summon Greatblade Phalanx blades overhead.

title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: Bols, Carian Knight deals Standard damage , Slash damage , Pierce damage , and Magic damage . Attack: Stomp, Description: Raises one leg and stomps repeatedly. Roll toward the leg that did not stomp and punish.; Attack: Swing, Description: Horizontal or vertical greatsword swings. Roll into horizontal; roll away from vertical.; Attack: Arm Sweep, Description: Low arm sweep along the ground. Roll into the arm.; Attack: Arm Slam, Description: One or both arms slam, producing a shockwave. Get behind him or jump the wave.; Attack: Downward Stab, Description: Drives the blade into the ground, large shockwave. Get behind him during the wind-up.; Attack: Sword Wrench, Description: Rips the sword free to his right after Downward Stab. Stay on his left.; Attack: Kneeling Swing, Description: Kneels and performs a wide counter-clockwise sweep.

title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: Roll toward the weapon.; Attack: Mega Slam, Description: Long hold overhead slam with huge shockwave. Safe spot is directly behind; or jump the wave.; Attack: Jumping Slam, Description: Leaps and lands with a shockwave. Roll on descent.; Attack: Dragging Slash, Description: Long wind-up dash dragging the blade, then slash and follow-ups ending in a slam. Slip between his legs or get behind him; luring him into the arena edge can stop and stun the charge.; Attack: Greatblade Phalanx, Description: Summons three blades above his head that fire after a delay. Run toward him so the blades overshoot; avoid pairing attempts since only one blade can be parried at a time.; Attack: Roar, Description: Shockwave roar that buffs his damage by 20% for ~15s. Get behind him if close; otherwise create distance.

title: Bols, Carian Knight
text: Use his size: dodge between his legs and stay on his back side. Keep him in view; he can pivot quickly into a follow-up. Stay close even for ranged builds; create distance only to reset spacing. When he casts Greatblade Phalanx , sprint toward him so the blades pass overhead. Shockwaves from slams fan out in front; positioning behind him mitigates most follow-ups.

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: The ice dragons were once lords of the mountaintops long ago, until they were defeated by the Fire Giants and chased from the peak. - Borealis's Mist Borealis the Freezing Fog is an optional Great Enemy boss in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Overview 2 Location 3 Drops 4 Moveset 5 Notes 6 Trivia 7 Gallery 8 See also 9 References

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Main article : Dragon Borealis the Freezing Fog was one of the ice dragons, who were lords of the Mountaintops before the Fire Giants chased them from the peak. [ 1 ] The dragon Borealis patrols the frozen expanse of the Freezing Lake in the northeastern Mountaintops of the Giants. [ 1 ] He emerges from a thick white fog, which he exhales clouds that inflict Frostbite .

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Borealis the Freezing Fog is located in the Mountaintops of the Giants , patrolling the frozen lake southeast of the Freezing Lake Site of Grace . The nearest Grace is Spiritcaller Cave .

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Defeating Borealis the Freezing Fog will award one Dragon Heart , 100,000 Runes , and will unlock Borealis's Mist , which can be obtained at the altar in the Cathedral of Dragon Communion in Caelid for two Dragon Hearts. Location: Mountaintops of the Giants ( Freezing Lake ), HP: 11,268, Runes: 100,000, Drops: Dragon Heart

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Instead of flames, Borealis exhales Dragonice and Borealis's Mist . Borealis inflicts Frostbite with his breath attacks and roars. His attacks include: Frost Breath: Borealis exhales a cone of freezing fog, which lingers and applies Frostbite . Frost Stomp: Stomps on the ground, creating a shockwave of ice that deals AoE damage and builds Frostbite . Frost Wing Sweep: Swings his massive wings, releasing icy projectiles that track players in the area. Roar: Releases a deafening roar that briefly staggers players and applies frost buildup.

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Borealis the Freezing Fog counts as a Dragon -type enemy and is vulnerable to anti-dragon effects. Furled Fingers and Spirit Ashes are allowed in this encounter. Defeating Borealis the Freezing Fog will unlock Borealis's Mist , which can be obtained at the altar in the Cathedral of Dragon Communion in Caelid for two Dragon Hearts .

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Borealis is the only ice dragon in the game.

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: This page has missing data (stats, moves). Please add it . The Cleanrot Knights vowed to fight alongside Malenia, despite the inevitable, if gradual, putrefaction of their flesh. Their acceptance of their fate made these battles the fiercest of all. - Cleanrot Helm Cleanrot Knights ( マレニアの貴腐騎士 , Marenia no Kifu Kishi ) are Enemies and Bosses in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Background 2 Chacteristics 3 Resistances 4 Locations 5 Bosses 6 Notes 7 Gallery 8 See Also 8.1 External Links 9 References

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: The Cleanrot Knights were fierce warriors who remained devoted to Malenia, Blade of Miquella , even as her scarlet rot consumed their bodies. [ 1 ] Their winged helms and sword insignias embodied the unyielding resolve of their Empyrean master. [ 2 ] They stood in arms with Miquella 's followers within Elphael, Brace of the Haligtree . They fought under Malenia's command throughout her undefeated campaign in the Shattering , [ 1 ] which culminated in the fateful clash against General Radahn 's Redmane army in Caelid . A majority of the knights fell at the Battle of Aeonia , their spirits locked in eternal combat in the War-Dead Catacombs . The few survivors wandered the resulting Swamp of Aeonia , while others traced the footsteps of Cleanrot Knight Finlay , who bore the sleeping Malenia all the way back to the Haligtree , [ 3 ] passing through the Shaded Castle along the way.

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: All Cleanrot Knights wear golden armor and wield a Cleanrot Knight's Sword as a secondary weapon to complement their polearms. [ 4 ] This allows them to land multiple attacks in quick succession, triggering the Talisman's blessing to strengthen them even further. Most Cleanrot Knights wield a Cleanrot Spear , which bears a small shield on its shaft. They will imbue their spear with gold before performing their more deadly attacks, such as grabbing their enemy with the spear tip and infecting them with Scarlet Rot, and summoning forth a golden palisade from the ground. [ 5 ] They can also brace their spear with their sword to absorb an incoming blow and quickly retaliate with their own. Cleanrot knight commanders wield the Halo Scythe . [ 6 ] They prefer ranged combat, repeatedly firing rings of Miquella's light from a distance. [ 7 ] In close quarters, they may vomit Scarlet Rot.

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: Cleanrot Knights are resistant to Slash and Holy damage, taking 35% and 40% less damage from these types. They receive flat damage from Pierce and Lightning damage, but take 20% extra damage from Fire attacks; however, the scythe-wielding variant can nullify this vulnerability by casting an incantation .
